title: narcissus radiiflorus var. exertus
intro: Narcissus radiiflorus var. exertus, also known as the autumn-flowering daffodil, is a plant species within the Amaryllidaceae family that is native to East Asia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including the presence of alkaloids such as galantamine, which has been shown to have potential in treating conditions like Alzheimer's disease. The health benefits of Narcissus radiiflorus var. exertus include its potential to improve cognitive function, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
